title: Social and humanities scientists
description: Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. All business operations specialists not listed separately. Develop, maintain, or implement business continuity and disaster recovery strategies and solutions, including risk assessments, business impact analyses, strategy selection, and documentation of business continuity and disaster recovery procedures. Plan, conduct, and debrief regular mock-disaster exercises to test the adequacy of existing plans and strategies, updating procedures and plans regularly. Act as a coordinator for continuity efforts after a disruption event. (Based on 36 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Write grants and proposals to obtain funding for research projects.
Develop, operate, and maintain geographical information systems, providing support to public and private sectors.
Collect and analyze data on physical characteristics of areas using various equipment and techniques.
Teach or mentor students in fields such as anthropology, archeology, geography, or sociology.
Study the cultural, political, and economic characteristics of populations, conducting fieldwork and research.
Gather and analyze data on human social behavior, collecting information through observations and interviews.
Prepare and present reports, publications, and exhibits containing research findings and analysis.
Advise individuals and organizations regarding social policies, research implications, and proposed programs.
Collaborate with other professionals to develop and evaluate methods for data collection and analysis.
Conduct participatory action research in communities to assess and design organizational systems and technologies.
Participate in public meetings to explain planning proposals, gather feedback from affected parties, or achieve consensus on project designs.
Produce environmental documents, such as environmental assessments or impact statements, and prepare reports or recommendations on transportation planning.
Review development plans for transportation system effects, infrastructure requirements, or compliance with transportation regulations and analyze traffic counting data.
Develop and test new methods or models of transportation analysis and design transportation surveys to identify public concerns.
Collaborate with engineers to research, analyze, or resolve complex transportation design issues while directing urban traffic counting programs.
Counsel individuals or groups to help them understand problems, deal with crises, and develop realistic action plans.
Develop and implement treatment plans based on clients' interests and needs, and document patient information including progress and recommendations.
Conduct psychological evaluations, select and interpret psychological tests, and prepare reports on individuals' psychological competence for various purposes.
Consult with other professionals, agencies, or community organizations about mental health resources, treatments, and patients' care.
Conduct research on neuropsychological disorders and develop new diagnostic or therapeutic counseling techniques based on findings.
Confer with information technology specialists to develop computer applications for genetic data analysis.
Instruct medical students, graduate students, or others in methods or procedures for diagnosis and management of genetic disorders.
Evaluate, diagnose, or treat genetic diseases and conduct family medical studies to evaluate the genetic basis for traits or diseases.
Verify that cytogenetic, molecular genetic, and related equipment is maintained in working condition to ensure accuracy and quality of experimental results.
Design sampling plans or coordinate the field collection of samples such as tissue specimens, and prepare or review reports, manuscripts, or meeting presentations.
Conduct applied research aimed at improvements in areas such as disease testing, pharmaceuticals, and the harnessing of microbes to recycle waste.
Conduct research on cell organization and function, including mechanisms of gene expression and cell signaling.
Collaborate with biologists and other professionals to conduct appropriate genetic and biochemical analyses.
Supervise or direct the work of other geneticists, biologists, or technicians working on genetics research projects.
Write grant applications to fund epidemiologic and biological research, and publish articles in scientific journals.
Communicate research findings on various types of diseases to health practitioners, policymakers, and the public.
Study effects of management practices, processing methods, feed, or environmental conditions on quality and quantity of animal products.
Investigate diseases or parasites to determine cause, risk factors, and mode of transmission and monitor and report incidents to health agencies.
Study the structure and function of human, animal, and plant tissues, cells, pathogens, and toxins, and conduct chemical analyses of substances.
Develop improved practices in feeding, housing, sanitation, or disease control of animals, and research animal nutrition and management.
Analyze characteristics of animals to identify and classify them, and conduct studies on their natural environments.
Prepare technical reports or recommendations based upon research outcomes and disseminate information through presentations.
Investigate genetic makeup and heritability of traits in animal populations and devise methods for altering traits.
Observe action of microorganisms upon living tissues of plants, higher animals, and dead organic matter.
Develop or update project plans including objectives, technologies, schedules, funding, and staffing.
Monitor costs incurred by project staff and identify budget issues.
Create project status presentations for delivery to customers or project personnel and report on project status.
Communicate with key stakeholders to determine project requirements and objectives and schedule project meetings.
Monitor project milestones and deliverables and request project updates to ensure deadlines are met.
Employ staff by recruiting or hiring project personnel and assigning duties or responsibilities to them.
Negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain necessary resources or materials.
Participate in online forums or conferences to stay updated on trends, techniques, or threats relevant to projects.
Produce and distribute project documents and prepare budget estimates or progress reports.
Confer with project personnel to identify and resolve problems related to project execution.
